Angola

Raw · Rich · Rising

Key facts

CapitalLuanda
Population34.5M
LanguagePortuguese
National sportFootball

About Angola

Angola is a country. Its capital city is Luanda, with other major cities including Huambo and Lubango. With a population of approximately 34.5M, the main language spoken is Portuguese.

Did you know

Angola has one of the world's youngest populations, with over 65% under age 25.

Explore Angola

🏛

Capital

Luanda serves as the political, cultural and economic heart of Angola. As the seat of government and often the most populous city, it concentrates the country's main institutions, universities and cultural landmarks. Beyond the capital, major cities include Huambo, Lubango, Benguela — each a hub of regional culture, economy and history. Luanda built one of Africa's most expensive skylines on oil money, its Marginal waterfront lined with glass towers that overlook Atlantic fishing villages where families still cure fish in the traditional Mbali style — a city of extreme contrasts compressed into a few city blocks.

👥

People

With a population of approximately 34.5M, Angola is a vibrant society with a rich mix of traditions and communities. The official language is Portuguese, which reflects the country's cultural heritage and connects it with a wide international community. Angolans carry the deep imprint of 27 years of civil war that ended in 2002 — a resilience expressed through kuduro music, a frantic dance genre born in Luanda's musseques (shanty towns) that became an international sensation without ever losing its local working-class roots.

🏅

Sport

Football holds a special place in the heart of Angola's national identity. Football is the dominant passion, but Angola's basketball programme has produced NBA players and continental championships, while the traditional Engolo martial art — practised by the Ngangela people — is widely considered the African ancestor of Brazilian capoeira.

⛰

Nature

The highest point in Angola is Moco, rising to 2,620 metres above sea level. The Tundavala Gap near Lubango is a dramatic escarpment dropping 1,000 metres from Angola's central plateau to the coastal plains below, while the remote Cuando Cubango province protects one of Africa's last intact elephant migration corridors straddling the Botswana border.

Major cities

Luanda Capital
Huambo
Lubango
Benguela
